Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 097 [Šumma Alu Excerpts][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo 6 firing holes
o 11

* TUG₂ NA GU₄ GU₇ NA BI ina-an-ziq

(o 1) (If) an ox eats a man's garment: that man will be annoyed.

o 22

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA GU₄ GU₇ DAM LU₂ BA.UŠ₂

(o 2) (If) an ox eats a man's cloak: the man's wife will die.

o 33

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA GU₄ GU₇ ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃<(DAM)> LU₂ BA.UŠ₂

(o 3) (If)an ox eats a man's girdle: ditto (the man's wife will die).

o 44

* TUG₂ NA UDU.NITA₂ GU₇ LU₂ BI TU.RA-ma TIuṭ

(o 4) (If) a ram eats a man's garment: that man will be ill, then recover.

o 55

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA UDU.NITA₂ GU₇ ZI.GA ina E₂ LU₂ E₃

(o 5) (If) a ram eats a man's cloak: loss will leave that man's house.

o 66

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA UDU.NITA₂ GU₇ DAM LU₂ in-nak BA.UŠ₂

(o 6) (If) a ram eats a man's girdle: that man's wife will have sexual intercourse (and) die.

o 77

* TUG₂ NA UD₅ GU₇ ḫi-ši-iḫ-ti : ḫi-pit LU₂ ina E₂ GAL₂

(o 7) (If) a goat eats a man's garment: desire (or): there will be a man-made breach in the house.

o 88

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA UD₅ GU₇ pa-an ḪULti : ḪUL.ŠA₃ ana NA GAL₂ši

(o 8) (If) a goat eats a man's cloak: face of evil (or): there will be sorrow for that man.

o 99

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA UD₅ GU₇ DAM LU₂ NIG₂.GIG IGImar

(o 9) (If) a goat eats a man's girdle: the man's wife will experience trouble.

o 1010

* TUG₂ ( o ) NA ŠAḪ GU₇ A₂.TUKU LU₂ TUR!(TU₄)er

(o 10) (If) a pig eats a man's garment: the man's profit will be reduced.

o 1111

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA ŠAḪ GU₇ NA BI ina NIG₂.GIG iš-pu-uʾ

(o 11) (If) a pig eats a man's cloak: that man will become silent in trouble.

o 1212

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA ŠAḪ GU₇ ( o ) DAM NA x

(o 12) (If) a pig eats a man's girdle: the man's wife [...].

o 1313

* TUG₂ NA ANŠE GU₇ ( o ) NA BI KI.ŠU₂ ina E₂ [...]

(o 13) (If) a donkey eats a man's garment: that man [...] (in) confinement in (his own) house.

o 1414

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA ANŠE GU₇ NA BI ana ki-di [...]

(o 14) (If) a donkey eats a man's cloak: that man [...] to the countryside.

o 1515

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA ANŠE GU₇ E₂-BI ( o ) BIRaḫ

(o 15) (If) a donkey eats a man's girdle: his household will be scattered.

o 1616

* TUG₂ NA ANŠE.KUR.RA GU₇ ( o ) NA BI ( o ) ŠU.SA₂.SA₂

(o 16) (If) a horse eats a man's garment: that man will be held tight.

o 1717

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA ANŠE.KUR.RA GU₇ NA BI ina ṭu₂-ub E₂-šu₂ E₃

(o 17) (If) a horse eats a man's cloak: that man will leave his prosperous house.

o 1818

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA ANŠE.KUR.RA GU₇ DAM LU₂ KI NITA₂ MAN-ma DAB-ma BA.UŠ₂

(o 18) (If) a horse eats a man's girdle: the man's wife will be caught with another male and will die.

o 1919

* TUG₂ NA UR.GI₇ GU₇ TUR GANBA ŠUBe E₂ LU₂

(o 19) (If) a dog eats a man's garment: reduction of business; abandonment of the man's house.

o 2020

* tug₂GU₂.E₃ NA UR.GI₇ GU₇ SU.GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂ši LU₂ BI KI.ŠU₂ DABsu NA BI ŠU!(SU) A.ZA.LU.LU

(o 20) (If) a dog eats a man's cloak: there will be famine in the land; captivity will befall that man; that man (will get) the hand of humanity.

o 2121

* tug₂IB₂.LA₂ NA UR.GI₇ GU₇ KISLAḪ₃ GARan-šu₂ ŠU!(SU) A.ZA.LU.LU i-ta-na-kal

(o 21) (If) a dog eats a man's girdle: distress will afflict him. The hand of humanity will keep hurting him.

o 2222

1.10 nis-ḫi * IRI ina SUKUD GARin BAR-MEŠ la₃ AL.TIL

(o 22) 70th excerpt of "If a city is located on a height"; non-canonical; not completed.

o 2323

* SIG₂ NA GU₄ GU₇ NA BI GIG dan-na <<GIG>> IGI

(o 23) (If) an ox eats a man's hem: that man will experience a severe illness.

o 2424

* SIG₂ NA UDU.NITA₂ GU₇ GIG dan-na IGI

(o 24) (If) a ram eats a man's hem: he will experience a severe illness.

o 2525

* SIG₂ NA UD₅ GU₇ KI.MIN<(GIG)> dan-na IGI

(o 25) (If) a goat eats a man's hem: ditto (he will experience a severe illness).

o 2626

* SIG₂ NA ŠAḪ GU₇ u₂-ta-zar

(o 26) (If) a pig eats a man's hem: he will be cursed.

o 2727

* SIG₂ NA ANŠE GU₇ mim-mu-šu₂ DUGUD E₃

(o 27) (If) donkey eats a man's hem: he will lose everything precious of his.

Reverse
rr some firing holes
r 11

* SIG₂ NA ANŠE.KUR.RA GU₇ KI.MIN<(mim-mu-šu₂)> DUGUD E₃

(r 1) (If) a horse eats a man's hem: ditto (he will lose everything precious of his).

r 22

* SIG₂ NA UR.GI₇ GU₇ IZI ina E₂ NA [...]

(r 2) (If) a dog eats a man's hem: a fire will [...] in the man's house.

r 33

* kušE.SIR₂ NA ⸢GU₄⸣ GU₇ SUḪ₃ ina KUR GARan NA BI [...]

(r 3) (If) an ox eats a man's sandal: chaos will be brought about in the land; that man [...].

r 44

* kušE.SIR₂ NA UDU.⸢NITA₂ GU₇⸣ BIRaḫ E₂ LU₂

(r 4) (If) a ram eats a man's sandal: scattering of the man's household.

r 55

* kušE.SIR₂ NA UD₅ GU₇ KI.MIN<(BIRaḫ)> E₂ LU₂

(r 5) (If) a goat eats a man's sandal: ditto (scattering of the man's household).

r 66

* kušE.SIR₂ NA ŠAḪ GU₇ DAM LU₂ BA.UŠ₂

(r 6) (If) a pig eats a man's sandal: the man's wife will die.

r 77

* kušE.SIR₂ NA ANŠE GU₇ ina la₃ DU₁₁-šu₂ DABbat

(r 7) (If) a donkey eats a man's sandal: he will be seized without (being able to) claim.

r 88

* kušE.SIR₂ NA ANŠE.KUR.RA GU₇ TU.RA-ma TIuṭ

(r 8) (If) a horse eats a man's sandal: he will be ill, then recover.

r 99

* kušE.SIR₂ NA UR.GI₇ GU₇ ZI.GA ṭu₂-ub-ba-a-tu₂ ina E₂ NA E₃

(r 9) (If) a dog eats a man's sandal: end of happiness; the man will leave (his) house.

2 firing holes on ruling
r 1010

1.11 nis-ḫi * IRI ina SUKUD GARin BAR-MEŠ la₃ AL.TIL

(r 10) 71th excerpt of "If a city is located on a height"; non-canonical; not completed.

r 1111

* e-ri-mu ina SAG!.DU LU₂ ⸢GAL₂!⸣ši NA BI i-lab-bi KUR₂.KUR₂

(r 11) (If) there is a mole (mark) on a man's head, that man will cry out; he will keep changing (his mind).

r 1212

1.12 nis-ḫi ( o o )

(r 12) 72th excerpt.

r 1313

* ⸢AB₂?⸣ MA GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ x KI RI x i-ta-ma-ma an-nu DABsu

(r 13) (If)... cows eat it: ... he will swear (an oath) with... and achieve his consent.

r 1414

1.13 nis-ḫi ( o o )

(r 14) 73rd excerpt.

1 firing hole on ruling
(colophon)
r 1515

* ⸢a?-bu? u₃?⸣ DUMU ki-it-mu-lu

(r 15) (If) a father and son are angry with one another.

1 line of 4 firing holes
r 1616

iš-tu gišDA GABA.RI UNUG!ki SAR-ma up-puš₄

(r 16) Written and properly executed from a writing board, copy of Uruk.

r 1717

IM mBAša₂-a bu-kur₂ mdINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir

(r 17) Tablet of Iqiša, son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir, incantation priest. Hand of Ištar-šum-ereš, his son.

2 firing holes
r 1818

lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ qat₃ mdINANA—MU—KAM DUMU-A.NI pa-liḫ da-nu₃

(r 18) He who reveres Anu and Antu shall not carry it off. May he return it to its owner on the same day. Month of Abu, 26th day, 6th year of Philip, king of all lands.

r 1919

u₃ an-tu₄ la₃ TUM₃-šu₂ ina u₄-mi-šu₂ a-na E₂ UMUN-šu₂

r 2020

ḪE₂.GUR-šu₂ itiNE.NE.GAR U₄ 26-KAM

r 2121

MU 6-KAM mpi-il-pi-is-su LUGAL KUR.KUR
